name: Porkbun API
what_it_does: REST API for managing domains registered with Porkbun — DNS records, domain registration, WHOIS updates, SSL certificates, and nameserver configuration.
use_cases: Programmatically creating and updating DNS records for deployed services
Automating domain registration and renewal for agent-managed infrastructure
Generating and installing free SSL certificates via Let's Encrypt through the API
Managing nameserver delegation for multi-domain projects
Checking domain availability and pricing before registration
not_for: Enterprise-grade DNS with advanced traffic management (use Route53 or Cloudflare instead)
High-volume DNS changes requiring millisecond propagation SLAs
best_when: An agent manages low-cost domain portfolios for personal projects, startups, or indie developers on a budget.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SLAs, advanced DNS features (geo-routing, load balancing), or support for all TLDs.

auth.notes: API key + secret key pair required for all requests. Keys generated in the Porkbun account dashboard. No fine-grained permission scopes — all keys have full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access is free; pay only for domains and SSL certs
pricing.paid_tiers: .com domains from ~$10-11/year; .io from ~$34/year; prices competitive vs market
pricing.notes: Porkbun is known for below-market domain pricing and free SSL. API itself has no cost.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; no official rate limit stated

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is functional but minimal. Error responses include a 'message' field but error codes are not comprehensive. Community-built clients exist for Python and Node.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: DNS record creation may fail if record already exists; no native upsert. Agents should check-then-create pattern.
known_agent_gotchas: No rate limit documentation - agents must implement conservative backoff to avoid being blocked
API key has full account access - no way to restrict to specific domains
DNS TTL changes take time to propagate regardless of API response
Some TLDs have registration restrictions not checked by the API before attempting

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key/secret model with no scope limitation is a security concern — a compromised key has full account control. No MFA option for API access.
